title: buildah from /path
body:
I was thinking it would be cool to be able to do `buildah from /PATH`.

The idea would be I could populate a directory or have a directory on my system that looks like a rootfs.  

buildah from /path 

Would figure out that the user specified a path (Command begins with /, ../, ./)

Then it would do the equivalent of

```
ctr=buildah from scratch
mnt=buildah mnt $ctr
cp -RP /PATH $mnt
buildah umount $ctr
```

comments.body:
One think buildah would need to do is make sure the STORAGE_DIR is not a subdir of /PATH, since this could get recursive.

`buildah from /`

Would be invalid.
SGTM, and I concur on the subpath check, that could get messy quick.

+1 on the proposal!

> Would figure out that the user specified a path (Command begins with /, ../, ./)

I wonder if that's a potential candidate for a new `raw:` image transport. I am not sure if that's something that should be fully supported by containers/image but adding something like a `raw:` transport could be a good placeholder for now. The benefit is that the interface of `buildah-from` wouldn't need to change in case `raw:` became a thing.

A `raw:` c/image transport would not be a great fit; c/image works with _immutable layers_ as single units, and more or less with random access to them, so dealing with exploded filesystems would be cumbersome and inefficient (even worse than dealing with tarballs of image layers).

E.g. an `ImageSource` would have to first create a ~layer tarball to know its digest, so that it can generate a manifest (consumers of images always start by reading the manifest before any other data); meanwhile, to avoid problems with the source directory concurrently changing, it’s pretty much necessary to store that tarball into a temporary file, and use _that_ as a source for a transport — and that already exists, as a `tarball:` transport, although it is also not all that efficient.

Of course all the components can be glued together, at the cost of several disk copies, digest computations, and the like — but all of that is more or less a reason _not_ to provide that glue in an easy to consume form, and instead nudge consumers towards creating complete images with full metadata as soon as possible in the process, allowing the images to be efficiently transferred/cached/consumed/combined.

---

The way I think about this, turning a directory structure into an image is the _primary purpose_ of buildah; it’s perfectly fine for a `buildah from-filesystem` or something like that [I don’t really want to get into bikeshedding but please think about ambiguities in string syntax with the `buildah from /path` proposal, as usual] to do an one-time costly operation which c/image does not otherwise make easy.

Also, don’t forget about all the metadata: a filesystem without a container configuration is not an image, and c/image does not provide any remotely user-friendly way to create such a configuration from scratch; yet the hypothetical `raw:` transport would have to provide _some_ configuration. Again, that’s what buildah is good at.
I am going to close this issue, since we have not made any progress in months.  Reopen if someone wants to work on it.
